question.text: Under SEBI regulations, what is the validity period of the NISM Series V-A: Mutual Fund Distributors Certification, and what is required for renewal?

answer.text: 3 years validity; renewal through CPE (Continuing Professional Education) or re-examination before expiry
5 years validity; no renewal required — lifetime certification
1 year validity; annual re-examination is mandatory
10 years validity; renewal through a simple online test

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The NISM Series V-A certification has a validity period of 3 years from the date of passing the examination. To renew, distributors must either: (1) complete the required Continuing Professional Education (CPE) credits before the certification expires, or (2) re-take and pass the NISM exam. If the certification lapses, the ARN also becomes invalid, and the distributor cannot earn commissions on new transactions until renewal is completed.
**The Trap:** It is NOT a lifetime certification — the 3-year renewal ensures distributors stay updated with regulatory changes. Annual re-examination is too frequent and not the actual requirement. The CPE route is the more common renewal path, as it does not require re-examination but involves attending approved training programs and accumulating the requisite CPE credits.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Certification requirements are tested in Series V-A (meta-question about your own exam!), V-B, and III-A. The CPE framework applies to all NISM certifications, not just Series V-A. Series XXIV (Compliance) tests the monitoring of certification validity for registered intermediaries.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Set a reminder at least 6 months before your certification expiry. CPE programs are offered by NISM, AMFI, and other approved providers. Treat CPE as a professional development opportunity, not just a compliance checkbox — the regulatory landscape changes frequently, and staying updated is essential for providing good service.
